creators.name: Nick Saraev
creators.blurb: Practical AI automation and agency builds from someone shipping real systems.

videos.title: I Built a $1M/y SaaS with Claude Code, Here's How
videos.summary: Nick Saraev describes building Clairvo, a $1M/year SaaS that uses Claude Code-generated techniques like predictive pacing and local number rotation to improve call pickup rates. He argues that using Claude to enumerate opportunities and practical implementation of AI agents is viable for building real-world revenue-generating businesses, contrasting his approach with many who talk automation without results.

videos.title: How to Build Mobile Apps with Claude Code: Full Course (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's full-length course teaches mobile app development using Claude Code and Expo/React Native. The course covers setup, app design frameworks, building three complete apps (habit tracker, calorie tracker with AI, Pomodoro timer), database integration with Supabase, user authentication, and app store submission.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

creators.name: AI Engineer
creators.blurb: Talks and sessions from the AI Engineer conference and community.

videos.title: GPU Cloud Deployment Without Leaving Your IDE — Audry Hsu, RunPod
videos.summary: Flash is a deployment framework that eliminates Docker/container iteration cycles by allowing developers to add a decorator to async Python functions and deploy to GPU cloud with hot reload. RunPod offers GPU cloud services starting with pods for experimentation and scaling to serverless autoscaling across data centers.

videos.title: RAG is dead, right?? — Kuba Rogut, Turbopuffer
videos.summary: Kuba Rogut argues RAG remains viable and useful despite "RAG is dead" discourse, noting Google search volume for RAG hit inflection point mid-2025. The real issue is narrow definition of retrieval. RAG encompasses vector search, full text search, glob, regex, and filters used iteratively by agents, with success depending on query volume and caching strategy like Cursor's semantic search showing measurable accuracy gains.

videos.title: From Transcription to Live Music: Gemini's Audio Stack — Thor Schaeff, Google DeepMind
videos.summary: Thor Schaeff from Google DeepMind demonstrates Gemini's audio stack, covering Gemini 3 Flash Preview for audio transcription with speaker labels, emotion detection, and language translation. The talk includes live demos of speech generation with director's notes and Gemini 3.1 Flash Live, a real-time multimodal audio model. It concludes with Lyria 3, a music generation model capable of producing full songs with lyrics via tool use.

videos.title: 2026 AI Engineer Vibe Reel
videos.summary: The AI Engineer channel is promoting the World's Fair event scheduled for San Francisco from June 29 to July 2, 2026. Viewers can purchase tickets and view the schedule at ai.engineer/wf.

creators.name: Cole Medin
creators.blurb: Practical AI agent and coding-assistant builds, end to end.

videos.title: Live AI Q&A + Crushing it in Chess at the Same Time - Come Hang Out!
videos.summary: Cole Medin is hosting a live AI Q&A session where viewers can ask questions about AI coding, agents, Claude Code, and Archon. The session combines technical discussion with casual chess gameplay and community building through Dynamous.

videos.title: Claude Plans, Gemini Designs: The Workflow to Build BEAUTIFUL Frontends
videos.summary: Cole Medin demonstrates a workflow for building frontends by composing Claude Opus and Gemini 3.5 Flash. The technique routes planning and reasoning tasks to Opus while delegating UI design and code generation to Gemini, which excels at visual frontend generation with lower cost. The workflow uses handoff documents to coordinate between models and includes verification layers.

videos.title: Claude Plans, Gemini Designs: One Workflow for Beautiful Frontends (LIVE)
videos.summary: Cole Medin demonstrates a multi-model AI workflow using Archon to chain Claude 3.5 Opus and Gemini 3.5 Flash for frontend development. Opus handles planning, copywriting, integrations and deployment while Gemini focuses on UI design, with Clerk CLI used for authentication integration.

videos.title: Harness Engineering: What Separates Top Agentic Engineers Right Now
videos.summary: Harness engineering is the practice of building the orchestration and control layer around coding agents, consisting of rules, skills, hooks, and subagents at the session level, and multi-agent workflow orchestration at scale. The harness is identified as roughly 98% of production agent systems like Claude Code, more important than the model itself.

creators.name: AI Jason
creators.blurb: AI app and agent builds, experiments, and walkthroughs.

videos.title: How to build proactive agents & self-improving company (Fully explained)
videos.summary: A video explaining how to build proactive agents and self-improving company systems. Covers proactive AI loops, autonomous optimization use cases, and tools like Loopany that enable memory and agent capabilities for autonomous decision-making.

videos.title: Ralph-loop 2.0? The real autonomous coder is coming...
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses emerging autonomous coding tools and features like /goal and /Mission exploration in coding assistants. The video covers how goal-based prompting enables more complex, horizontal task execution compared to traditional coding workflows.

videos.title: New AI coding paradiagm - OpenAI Symphony
videos.summary: OpenAI Symphony is presented as a new AI coding paradigm that enables automated testing and workflow setup for AI-assisted development. The video covers Symphony's functionality, implementation via harness setup, and integration with WORKFLOW.md files.

videos.title: Okay, this unleashed my agent
videos.summary: AI Jason presents a video overview of AI agent capabilities including autoresearch, autoagent, Claude Code's memory architecture, Hermes self-evolution, and Openclaw skill evolution. The video references Crewlet as a key tool and includes timestamps for different agent features and mechanisms.

creators.name: Caleb Writes Code
creators.blurb: Hands-on coding sessions and software build-alongs.

videos.title: MiniMax M3 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: MiniMax releases M3, a language model implementing MiniMax Sparse Attention (MSA) to replace full attention mechanisms. The model includes tiled KV cache optimizations and I/O improvements that reduce memory bandwidth bottlenecks during prefill and decode stages of inference.

videos.title: Pi Agent explained in 6min..
videos.summary: A video explaining Pi Agent, positioning it as a unique approach that opens new use cases. The content compares Pi against frontier agents like Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, and Antigravity, particularly in how it handles complexity in the harness layer as products ship with more features.

videos.title: Huawei makes 1.4nm chip??
videos.summary: Caleb analyzes Huawei's announcement of the Tau Scaling Law as a competitive response in the hardware and semiconductor race between China and the USA. The video examines the role of EUV and DUV lithography, packaging, and logic folding in advancing semiconductor capabilities.

videos.title: Agent Harness explained in 8min..
videos.summary: A video explaining agent harness engineering as a foundational concept in agentic AI systems. The content traces the evolution from prompt engineering and context engineering through the development of harness engineering, covering loops and system architecture.

creators.name: AWS Developers
creators.blurb: Official AWS tutorials, service deep dives, and developer demos.

videos.title: Host any coding agent on AgentCore
videos.summary: AWS announced the ability to host and run multiple coding agents including Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, and Kiro on Amazon Bedrock AgentCore. This enables developers to deploy various coding agents on a unified platform.

videos.title: 5 Tips for Deploying AI Agents to Production
videos.summary: AWS tutorial covering five production best practices for TypeScript AI agents: streaming tool call events for UI responsiveness, multi-layer authentication and rate limiting, type-safe database access with tenant isolation, agent guardrails with cost budgets and model routing, and observability via metrics and distributed tracing. Examples use Strands SDK, AWS services, and OpenTelemetry.

videos.title: Infrastructure as Code for Beginners (AWS CDK)
videos.summary: AWS Developers tutorial on Infrastructure as Code fundamentals using AWS CDK with Python. The video contrasts manual AWS console setup (ClickOps) of serverless components (DynamoDB, Lambda, API Gateway) against building the same REST API infrastructure programmatically with CDK, demonstrating deployment, testing, and teardown workflows.

videos.title: Stop AI Hallucinations With These 5 Techniques
videos.summary: AWS Developers video presents five techniques to reduce hallucinations and token waste in AI agents: semantic tool selection to filter context, graph RAG for precise structured queries, multi-agent validation to catch failures, symbolic guardrails enforced in code, and runtime steering for self-correction. The techniques are demonstrated on a travel agent and integrated in Amazon Bedrock AgentCore.

creators.name: Visual AI
creators.blurb: Visual walkthroughs and demos across modern AI tools.

creators.name: Alberta Tech
creators.blurb: Developer tutorials and practical builds in AI and software.

videos.title: How Meta AI accidentally gave away Obama's instagram
videos.summary: Meta AI inadvertently exposed or compromised former President Barack Obama's Instagram account. The incident represents a security failure by Meta's AI systems. Alberta Tech describes the incident in a video commentary on YouTube.

videos.title: Do Google engineers actually vibe code?
videos.summary: Alberta Tech examines whether Google engineers adopt code generation ("vibing code"), comparing OpenAI Codex and Claude Code, and projects Google's code generation strategy through 2026. The video sections span current practices at Google and forward-looking technical directions.

videos.title: CEO finds out tokens cost money
videos.summary: A video from Alberta Tech presents a CEO's realization about the cost implications of token-based pricing in AI systems. The piece appears to be commentary on business economics of language model usage.

videos.title: He's a marketing "engineer"!? 🤨
videos.summary: A YouTube video from Alberta Tech that questions or expresses skepticism about someone using the title "marketing engineer", suggesting doubt about the legitimacy or appropriateness of the designation.

creators.name: EO
creators.blurb: Conversations and stories with founders and builders in tech.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Know if Your Product Market Fit Is Real | Serval CEO, Jake Stauch
videos.summary: Jake Stauch, CEO of Serval, argues that rabid fans and passion do not indicate real product-market fit. He emphasizes that genuine PMF manifests as customer pull rather than founder push, and advises embedding in customers' work and Slack channels instead of conducting formal interviews.

videos.title: He Raised $26M Betting on Probabilities, Not Guesses | Koah Labs, Nic Baird
videos.summary: Nic Baird, founder of Koah Labs, argues that subscription models have failed for AI apps and that advertising is the viable monetization path forward. He positions the primary bottleneck for AI agents as human trust adoption rather than technology limitations.

videos.title: I Hit $1M ARR in 117 Days. Bootstrapped to $10M. Here's My Playbook | Chatbase, Yasser Elsaid
videos.summary: Yasser Elsaid of Chatbase shares his bootstrapping strategy that achieved $1M ARR in 117 days and grew to $10M ARR without external funding. The interview covers product-led growth, churn reduction, SEO strategy, pricing experimentation, and decision frameworks for bootstrapped founders.

videos.title: A CS Professor on Why Slow Learning Wins in the AI Era | CU Boulder, Tom Yeh
videos.summary: Tom Yeh, an Associate Professor at CU Boulder, argues that teaching AI by hand at human speed with focus on mathematical foundations produces durable skills that survive hype cycles. He contends that understanding foundational concepts is more valuable than chasing the latest tools, and that people must shape AI rather than be shaped by it.

creators.name: Cyril Imhof
creators.blurb: AI automation and workflow builds for real projects.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5: The New AI That Changes Everything! #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof presents Claude Fable 5.0 as a new AI model distinct from Opus, highlighting the "Know Your Agent" concept as having significant impact on the AI landscape.

videos.title: Anthropic's Brand New AI Just Started the Gold Rush (Fable 5)
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof reviews Anthropic's newly released Claude Fable 5 model and discusses emerging AI use cases. The video covers agent design patterns and context engineering for AI applications.

videos.title: Unlock AI Agent Control: Gemma 4, Local Power & Your Questions Answer #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses updates on local AI agent control with Gemma 4 and invites audience questions for future content. The channel is positioning itself toward AI builders interested in on-device AI control.

videos.title: AI is Going LOCAL! Edge AI & Local Agents Explained #shorts
videos.summary: A video discussing the rise of edge AI and local agents as a paradigm shift in data-sensitive industries. The commentary emphasizes that edge deployment offers security, privacy, and computational capability advantages for finance and healthcare applications.

creators.name: PY
creators.blurb: Software engineering, tooling, and developer-workflow deep dives.
